_comment: DEMO ONLY — learning exercise for module-09. Not a real global hook.

hooks.command: bash -c 'if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -q "git commit"; then cd module-09 && python -m pytest tests/ -q --tb=no 2>&1; STATUS=$?; if [ $STATUS -ne 0 ]; then echo "HOOK BLOCKED: pytest failed (exit $STATUS). Fix tests before committing."; exit 2; fi; fi'
hooks._what_it_does: Intercepts any Bash call that contains 'git commit'. Runs pytest in module-09/tests/. If pytest exits non-zero, the hook prints a message and returns exit code 2, which causes Claude Code to block the tool call and show the error to the user.
